q: Consider the equation H = x p \epsilon q $E_{r}$ t s Where H = magnetic field; E = electric field, \epsilon = permittivity, x = distance, t = time The values of p , q , r and s respectively are :
explanation: To find the values of the exponents p , q , r , and s , we will use dimensional analysis. Both sides of the equation must have the same fundamental dimensions. The dimension of distance ( x ) is the dimension of length [ x ] = L The dimension is time [ t ] = T The dimension of electric field ( E ) can be derived from the formula for force on a charge, F = q E . Therefore, E = F / q . The dimensions of force is [ F ] = $[MLT^{-2} ]$ Charge ( q ) = Current \times Time So, the dimensions of charge is, [ q ] = $[AT ]$ So, the dimensions of electric field is, [ E ] = $[MLT^{-2} ]$ $[AT ]$ = $[MLT^{-3} A^{-1} ]$ Using relationship between electric field (E) and and magnetic field (H) in electromagnetic waves. In a vacuum, the ratio of the magnitudes of the electric field to the magnetic field (B) is the speed of light (c), E / B = c Using the formula of magnetic force on a current-carrying conductor: F = BIL Where, F = force B = magnetic field (flux density), I = current and L = length B = F IL Substituting the dimensions, [ B ] = $[MLT^{-2} ]$ $[LA ]$ â‡’ [ B ] = [ M T - 2 I - 1 ] The relation between B and H is B = \mu H â‡’ B \mu = H . Where \mu = permeability. Using the formula of magnetic field, B = \mu i 2 \pi r â‡’ [ B \mu ] = [ i r ] , here r is distance from straight conductor carrying current i. [ B \mu ] = [ H ] = $[A ]$ $[L ]$ = $[L^{-1} A ]$ Putting the dimensions of electric field and speed of light, the dimensions of magnetic field is, [ H ] = $[MLT^{-3} A^{-1} ]$ $[LT^{-1} ]$ = $[MT^{-2} A^{-1} ]$ We can derive the dimensions of permittivity ( \epsilon ) from Coulomb's Law, F = 1 4 \pi \epsilon q 1 q 2 r 2 . [ \epsilon ] = [ q 2 Fr 2 ] [ \epsilon ] = $[AT ]$ 2 $[MLT^{-2} ]$ $[L^{2}]$ = $[M^{-1} L^{-3} T^{4}A^{2}]$ Substituting the dimensions into the given equation, [ H ] = [ x ] p [ \epsilon ] q [ E ] r [ t ] s â‡’ $[L^{-1} A ]$ = $[L ]$ p $[M^{-1} L^{-3} T^{4}A^{2}]$ q $[M L T^{-3} A^{-1} ]$ r $[T ]$ s $[L^{-1} A ]$ = [ M - q + r L p - 3 q + r T 4 q - 3 r - s A 2 q - r ] On comparing the powers of M , L , T , and A on both sides, For M , - q + r = 0 â€¦ (i) For A , 2 q - r = 1 . Since r = q , then 2 q - q = 1 , so q = 1 and r = 1 . For L , p - 3 q + r = - 1 â€¦ (ii). p - 3 ( 1 ) + 1 = - 1 p - 2 = - 1 â‡’ p = 1 For T, 4 q - 3 r - s = 0 â€¦ (iii) 4 ( 1 ) - 3 ( 1 ) - s = 0 1 - s = 0 â‡’ s = 1 Therefore, the values are p = 1 , q = 1 , r = 1 , s = 1 . Hence, the correct option is (A).
